bead.title: Add cross-repo / downstream-consumer verification to the verification concern
bead.description:
PROBLEM
The verification concern's 'done != usable' contract covers a single system end-to-end (and the recently-hardened runtime-boundary list: MCP handshake, OAuth/SSO, real-network). It does NOT cover the case where a change has a known DOWNSTREAM CONSUMER in another repo. Observed papering: a niflheim<->heimq integration where an agent reported 'landed on main / gate green' but the human had to manually verify the consumer ('pull heimq and find out', 'check for the missing seams'). The human became the cross-repo integration layer. This is a FEAT-016 (artifact honesty / done!=usable) gap.

CURRENT STATE (verified)
- grep for downstream|consumer|cross-repo in workflows/concerns/verification/concern.md and practices.md returns nothing. The concern's exercise scope is single-system ('the full stack from user action to datastore and back').

PROPOSED FIX
- Add a Constraint and a Drift Signal to workflows/concerns/verification/concern.md: when a change has a named downstream consumer, 'done' requires EVIDENCE THAT THE DOWNSTREAM CONSUMER STILL WORKS (the consumer pulled the change and its integration was exercised) — not just local gate-green.
- Add a matching bullet to workflows/concerns/verification/practices.md.
- Add a manual exit item to workflows/activities/04-build/GATE.yaml mirroring it.
- Scope NARROWLY to 'evidence the named downstream consumer works' — do NOT prescribe a test strategy (testing concern boundary).
- Mirror to the shipped skill tree: run python3 scripts/sync_references.py so skills/helix/references/ matches workflows/ (validate-skills.sh fails on drift).

IN-SCOPE FILES
- workflows/concerns/verification/concern.md
- workflows/concerns/verification/practices.md
- workflows/activities/04-build/GATE.yaml
- skills/helix/references/** (regenerated by scripts/sync_references.py — do not hand-edit)

OUT-OF-SCOPE (file a separate bead)
- workflows/actions/converge.md, fresh-eyes-review.md, scope-discipline concern
- Any new concern or action
- Integration-test mechanics (testing concern owns that)

PROVENANCE
Derived from a session-harvest of local Claude/Codex operator prompts (2026-06-27), classified deficiency-papering vs intent, then put through two adversarial-review rounds that dropped every item already shipped in helix@40d6920b. This is the one HELIX-ownable gap that survived: cross-repo/downstream-consumer verification is genuinely absent from the verification concern.
bead.acceptance: 1. grep -ri 'downstream\|consumer' workflows/concerns/verification/concern.md returns the new constraint, and practices.md has the matching bullet. 2. workflows/activities/04-build/GATE.yaml has a new manual exit item for downstream-consumer verification. 3. python3 scripts/sync_references.py then git diff --quiet skills/helix/references (no drift). 4. just test passes.
